page: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at 2.4 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity stands at 768.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.

Storage is a core feature of this instance, which includes 22500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps. The CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8), which accelerate cryptographic, vector, and deep learning operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7i.24xlarge demonstrates strong to top-tier performance. Multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core, rank in the top 25% and top 10% respectively. Decompression tasks via multi-threaded bzip3 and static web serving throughput both achieve top-tier status. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only small block cached transfers reaching 6647.85 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are strong, registering 113.26 million operations per second.

This hardware profile offers high resource density for data-intensive workloads. The combination of massive local NVMe storage, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced CPU instructions makes it highly cost-efficient for large-scale databases, real-time analytics, and high-throughput web serving, without requiring external network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory. The server is equipped with 22500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core processing, static web serving throughput, and multi-threaded decompression. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for cryptography and matrix operations. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, real-time data analytics, and high-volume web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity storage-optimized server featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity, fast DDR5 memory, and advanced Intel Xeon multi-core processing.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 320 MB L3 cache
768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
22500 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated workloads
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and high-throughput web serving
